title: Houston Endowment General Grants
description: Houston’s largest private foundation funding public education, civic engagement, arts/culture, and greenspaces. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Education, Arts & Culture, Environment, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Greater Houston, TX — Harris County + 7 contiguous counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) public charities serving Greater Houston. Award: not standardized, up to $10M. Deadline: rolling/invitation-based (unsolicited LOI accepted).

title: Houston Endowment Collaboration Fund
description: Helps current grantees strengthen organizational capacity — staffing, operations, tech. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Greater Houston, TX). Eligibility: Current HEI grantees with smaller operating budgets. Award: up to $75,000. Deadline: rolling, invitation-only.

title: Meadows Foundation Grants
description: Major statewide TX funder across education, environment, homelessness, mental health, arts, and nonprofit capacity-building. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Education, Environment, Mental Health, Arts & Culture, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Statewide (Texas). Eligibility: Texas 501(c)(3) nonprofits. Award: varies. Deadline: rolling.

title: Greater Houston Community Foundation — High-Impact Grantmaking
description: Funds organizations tackling intergenerational poverty and economic mobility in Houston. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Greater Houston, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) via open LOI call. Award: ~$200,000. Deadline: fixed date (LOI cycle; check ghcf.org for next opening).

title: Moody Foundation Grants
description: Major TX family foundation; funds museums, parks, healthcare, education statewide. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Arts & Culture, Community Development, Education. Geographic scope: Statewide (Texas). Eligibility: Texas 501(c)(3) nonprofits. Award: $50,000–$1,000,000+. Deadline: rolling.

title: RWJF Exploring Equitable Futures
description: Seeds unconventional ideas advancing health equity long-term. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: U.S. nonprofits/organizations. Award: no set amount (project-based). Deadline: annual (closes ~Oct 15, reopens ~Feb 1; check rwjf.org).

title: RWJF From Insight to Action: Health Equity Research
description: Funds community-rooted health equity research via HERA program; focuses on 7 prioritized systems including housing, education, food, and criminal-legal systems. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Current/former RWJF grantees with 2+ year community partnerships; community-based org must serve as co-PI. Award: up to $500,000/project ($8M total pool; ~20 grants). Deadline: Full proposals due July 27, 2026 at 3pm ET (LOI closed May 14; invited applicants only at this stage; new cycle expected 2027).

title: RWJF Local Data for Equitable Communities
description: Funds local data collection/use to address place-based inequities. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: U.S. 501(c)(3) or 501(c)(4) nonprofits. Award: $50,000 (~30 grants). Deadline: fixed date (full proposals due March 3, 2026; watch rwjf.org for next cycle).

title: RWJF Health Equity Scholars for Action (HES4A)
description: Supports underrepresented faculty conducting health equity research. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Education. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Early-career researchers via host institutions. Award: up to $260,000. Deadline: annual (LOI due ~Nov 6 annually; check rwjf.org for 2026–2027 cycle).

title: Petco Love Spay/Neuter & Veterinary Care Grant
description: Annual September grant window for veterinary access organizations; funds spay/neuter programs, community cat management, and pet care financial assistance for families. Funder type: nonprofit. Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Spay/neuter clinics, full-service low-cost veterinary care providers, TNR/community cat organizations, and organizations providing financial aid for family pets. Award: varies. Deadline: UPCOMING — September 1–30, 2026 (confirmed on petcolove.org/shelter-partners/grants; apply via Petco Love Partner Portal).

title: Petco Love Stories Grant Campaign
description: Story-based campaign rewarding adoption-focused organizations; $8M+ distributed since 2013. Funder type: nonprofit. Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) animal welfare orgs with completed Annual Data Report. Award: up to $100,000. Deadline: UPCOMING — August–October 2026 (confirmed on petcolove.org/shelter-partners/grants as upcoming application period).

title: PetSmart Charities Adoption Growth Grant
description: Supports growing in-store adoption programs in PetSmart stores. Funder type: nonprofit (corporate-affiliated). Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: National (US, Canada, Puerto Rico). Eligibility: Active PetSmart Charities Adoption Partners w/ min. in-store adoptions completed. Award: up to $75,000. Deadline: fixed date (Oct 6–Nov 6 annually; check petsmartcharities.org for 2026 dates).

title: Walmart Spark Good Local Grants
description: Local grants from individual Walmart/Sam’s Club facilities for community needs. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Food Security. Geographic scope: National (store-level). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) public charities, K-12 schools, churches w/ community projects in Walmart/Sam’s Club service areas. Award: $250–$5,000. Deadline: rolling.

title: RWJF Global Ideas for U.S. Solutions
description: Funds U.S. health equity solutions inspired by international approaches. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: U.S.-based organizations (international partners allowed). Award: project grants (amount varies). Deadline: fixed date (brief proposals due April 13, 2026; watch rwjf.org for next cycle).

title: RWJF Health Policy Fellows
description: Places health professionals in federal policy fellowships. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Health professionals/individuals via sponsoring institutions. Award: fellowship stipend. Deadline: annual (preliminary applications due ~Nov 3 annually; check rwjf.org for 2026–2027 cycle).

title: PetSmart Charities Access to Veterinary Care Grants
description: Funds programs expanding access to affordable veterinary care. Funder type: nonprofit. Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: National (US, Canada). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, municipalities. Award: varies (part of $100M 5-year commitment). Deadline: rolling/cyclical (check petsmartcharities.org/pro/grants for open windows).

title: PetSmart Charities Pet Food Assistance Grants
description: Supports pet food insecurity programs so families don’t have to choose between feeding themselves and their pets. Funder type: nonprofit. Focus areas: Animal Welfare, Food Security. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits and municipalities operating pet food programs for families facing economic hardship. Award: varies (part of $11M+ donated in pet food in 2024). Deadline: cyclical (check petsmartcharities.org/pro/grants for open windows).

title: NEA Grants for Arts Projects (GAP) — July 2026 Cycle
description: NEA’s principal grant program; projects begin no earlier than June 1, 2027. Both parts must be submitted — missing Part 1 disqualifies Part 2 submission. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) US nonprofits w/ 5+ years arts programming, $20,000+ operating expenses; units of gov’t; tribes. Award: $10,000–$100,000 (Local Arts Agencies subgranting: $30,000–$150,000); 1:1 match required. Deadline: UPCOMING — Part 1 (Grants.gov) due July 9, 2026 at 11:59pm ET; Part 2 (NEA Applicant Portal) due July 21, 2026 at 11:59pm ET.

title: NEA Our Town
description: Supports creative placemaking arts projects integrated with community development; government partner required. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Community Development. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, local governments, tribes (apply via GAP, choose “Design & Our Town” discipline; partnership between arts org and government entity required). Award: $10,000–$100,000 (1:1 match required). Deadline: UPCOMING — July 9, 2026 (Part 1); July 21, 2026 (Part 2) (aligned with GAP July cycle; same calendar).

title: NEA Research Grants in the Arts
description: Funds research studies investigating value/impact of the arts in American life. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) US nonprofits w/ 5+ years operating history; tribes. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (March cycle; check arts.gov for FY2028 cycle announcement; noted on arts.gov as “deadline has passed” for current year).

title: NEA Research Labs
description: Funds long-term empirical research agendas on arts’ societal contributions. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, STEM & Technology. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: US higher-ed institutions or 501(c)(3) nonprofits w/ 5-year research history. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (March cycle aligned with Research Grants; check arts.gov for next solicitation).

title: Sports Matter Program (DICK’S Sporting Goods Foundation)
description: Funds equipment, registration fees, league costs for under-resourced youth sports programs. Join the Sports Matter Community mailing list to be notified of invitation opportunities. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Sports & Recreation, Youth Programs. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving youth ≤18 in zip codes w/ 15%+ poverty rate, or public/charter schools w/ 40–50%+ free/reduced lunch. Award: $1,000–$25,000. Deadline: Invitation-only as of 2026 (per sportsmatter.org/s/apply-for-funding; join Sports Matter Community email list at sportsmatter.org to receive grant opportunity notifications when windows open; $15.9M+ distributed in 2024; currently building invitation pipeline).

title: DICK’S Sporting Goods Foundation Multi-Year Partnership Program
description: Multi-year tiered funding plus capacity-building support; 9 organizations selected in 2025; future cohorts may be announced via sportsmatter.org. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Sports & Recreation, Youth Programs, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (9 markets: Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Durham, Houston, LA, Miami, NYC, Pittsburgh). Eligibility: Selected youth sports nonprofits in designated markets (highly selective; selected by Foundation in partnership with LISC and Good Sports). Award: $175,000 over 3 years ($100K in 2025, $50K in 2026, $25K in 2027). Deadline: invitation-only.

title: Walmart Foundation Local Community Grant Program
description: Local Walmart/Sam’s Club facilities fund nonprofits addressing local community needs. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Environment, Healthcare, Food Security. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in 8 funding areas near Walmart/Sam’s Club facilities. Award: $250–$5,000. Deadline: rolling.

title: USDA Community Food Projects Competitive Grants Program (CFP)
description: One-time federal infusion to make community food security projects self-sustaining. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Food Security. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Private nonprofits, public food program service providers, tribal organizations. Award: $125,000–$400,000 over up to 4 years (1:1 match required). Deadline: annual (historically October; watch nifa.usda.gov for FY2027 NOFO).
funder: USDA National Institute of Food and Agriculture (NIFA)

title: USDA CFPCGP Planning Projects
description: Planning grants for orgs building toward full community food security projects. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Food Security. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Private nonprofits, tribal organizations, food program service providers. Award: $25,000–$50,000 over 12–36 months (1:1 match required). Deadline: annual (historically October; aligned with CFP cycle; watch nifa.usda.gov for FY2027 NOFO).
funder: USDA National Institute of Food and Agriculture

title: Feeding America Food Security Equity Impact Fund
description: Funds community-led, systemic hunger solutions; 90% of grantees are BIPOC-led. Funder type: nonprofit. Focus areas: Food Security. Geographic scope: National (US + Puerto Rico). Eligibility: Nonprofits/food program partners connected to Feeding America network food banks; priority to BIPOC-led orgs. Award: $250,000–$500,000 (multi-year; $20M+ awarded since 2021 to 75+ orgs). Deadline: rolling/cyclical (partnership with food bank required; check feedingamerica.org).

title: Chick-fil-A True Inspiration Awards — 2028 Cycle (Opens March 2027)
description: Annual program in 4 categories: Caring for People, Caring through Food, Caring for Communities, Caring for Our Planet. Register at chick-fil-a.com to receive notifications when 2028 cycle opens. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Education, Food Security, Community Development, Environment. Geographic scope: National (US, Puerto Rico, Canada, UK, Singapore). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) US/Puerto Rico nonprofits; orgs that received 2026 or 2027 awards are ineligible. Award: $30,000–$350,000 (16 grants; $6M total annually). Deadline: UPCOMING — March 1–May 1, 2027 (2027 cycle closed May 1, 2026; 2028 cycle application details expected early 2027 at chick-fil-a.com/true-inspiration-awards/learn-more).

title: Kresge Foundation — Arts & Culture Grants
description: Funds culture and creativity as drivers of just communities; general operating and project grants. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: National (priority: Detroit, Memphis, New Orleans, Fresno). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits; primarily invited; check kresge.org/grants-social-investments/current-funding-opportunities for open calls. Award: $5,000–$5,000,000. Deadline: rolling/invitation-based.

title: Kresge Foundation — Education Grants
description: Funds postsecondary attainment, minority-serving institutions, community colleges, and urban pathways to college. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Education. Geographic scope: National (focus states: CA, FL, MI, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, colleges/universities; not accepting unsolicited proposals. Award: $5,000–$5,000,000+. Deadline: invitation-based.

title: Kresge Foundation — Environment Grants
description: Helps cities develop equitable climate resilience strategies; clean energy, water equity, climate/health equity. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Environment. Geographic scope: National (urban climate resilience focus). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits working on climate resilience; primarily invited. Award: varies (multi-year grants common). Deadline: invitation-based.

title: Kresge Foundation — Human Services Grants
description: Increases economic mobility for people with low incomes; single- and multi-year general operating and project grants. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Community Development. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits increasing socioeconomic mobility for low-income people; primarily invited. Award: varies. Deadline: invitation-based.

title: San Antonio Area Foundation Collaborative Grants
description: Trust-based grantmaking closing opportunity gaps for San Antonio-area residents. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Education, Community Development, Health. Geographic scope: Regional (Bexar County + 7 contiguous counties, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits providing programs directly benefiting Bexar County and surrounding area. Award: varies (see 2026 Grant Guidance on site). Deadline: annual (multiple cycles; check saafdn.org for next window).

title: San Antonio Area Foundation Capital Grants
description: Capital grants supporting infrastructure and physical capacity improvements for nonprofits in San Antonio region. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Bexar County + surrounding counties, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in the greater San Antonio region. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (check saafdn.org for current cycle).

title: Austin Community Foundation — Forever Austin Fund
description: ACF’s flagship endowment-funded grant program; unrestricted general operating support. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Community Development, Economic Mobility, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Central Texas: Travis, Williamson, Hays, Bastrop, Burnet, Caldwell counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or fiscally sponsored projects with $50,000+ budget; 2+ years operating. Award: general operating support ($2.3M awarded to 99 orgs in 2025 cycle). Deadline: annual (check austincf.org for 2027 cycle opening; LOI opens Jan. 2027).

title: Austin Community Foundation — Community Grants
description: Project-specific grants funded by donor-advised fundholders. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Multi-sector (8 impact areas). Geographic scope: Regional (Central Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in Central Texas; apply once per 12-month period. Award: $1,000–$75,000 (avg. ~$25,000; $2M+ awarded annually via fundholders). Deadline: quarterly (rolling quarterly review; apply 4–5 months before program start; check austincf.org).

title: Austin Community Foundation — Hispanic Impact Fund
description: Funds nonprofits advancing economic mobility for Central Texas’s Latino community; bilingual applications accepted. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (6-county Central Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or units of government serving the 6-county region. Award: unrestricted general operating support ($550,000 total to 16 orgs annually). Deadline: Full application due July 31, 2026 (LOI closed June 1; full apps still open through July 31; check austincf.org for 2027 cycle dates).

title: Austin Community Foundation — Women’s Fund
description: Funds nonprofits advancing economic mobility for women and families in Central Texas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education. Geographic scope: Regional (Central Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in 6-county Central Texas. Award: unrestricted general operating support. Deadline: annual (check austincf.org for 2026/2027 cycle).

title: Austin Community Foundation — Black Fund
description: Funds nonprofits advancing equity and opportunity for Black residents of Central Texas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education. Geographic scope: Regional (Central Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in Central Texas. Award: unrestricted general operating support. Deadline: annual (check austincf.org for current cycle).

title: BISSELL Pet Foundation — Partners for Pets Grant Program
description: BISSELL Pet Foundation’s core grant network; funds adoption, spay/neuter, transport, and emergency response for 6,025+ animal welfare partner organizations nationwide. Funder type: nonprofit (corporate-affiliated). Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: National (all 50 US states). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or public local government animal welfare orgs; must be Partners for Pets network members for 6+ months prior to applying; must actively work on spay/neuter, microchipping, and/or adoption; must microchip all adopted animals. Award: varies by program (adoption, spay/neuter, transport, crisis/disaster, vaccines). Deadline: rolling/event-based (year-round programs including Empty the Shelters adoption events, Fix the Future spay/neuter clinics, and crisis/disaster response; join Partners for Pets network at bissellpetfoundation.org/grant-information/partners-for-pets first; 6,025+ current partner organizations).

title: USDA Community Food Projects — Planning Projects (Separate Track)
description: Planning grants for orgs building toward full community food security projects; includes food access, urban farming, and food systems strategy. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Food Security. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Private nonprofits, tribal organizations, public food program service providers. Award: $25,000–$50,000 (1:1 match required). Deadline: annual (historically October; watch nifa.usda.gov for FY2027 NOFO announcement).
funder: USDA / National Institute of Food and Agriculture

title: ASPCA — Grants for Animal Welfare Organizations
description: Funds anti-cruelty law enforcement, sheltering innovations, spay/neuter access, equine welfare, and disaster response for animals; also funds research and policy work. Funder type: nonprofit. Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) animal welfare organizations, humane societies, and municipal shelters; must align with ASPCA grant priorities. Award: $10,000–$500,000 (ASPCA grants $10M+ annually; $200M+ to 4,000+ orgs since 2001). Deadline: varies by program (check aspca.org/about-us/aspca-grants for current open programs; multiple grant tracks open at different times of year; LA Animal Services received $14M in April 2026).

title: Communities Foundation of Texas — Community Grants
description: Largest community foundation in Dallas and the Southwest by grant volume; $3B+ awarded since 1953. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Health, Community Development, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (North Texas / Dallas metro). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) with 2+ years active designation; serving North Texas; complete Nonprofit Gateway Profile. Award: varies (CFT distributed $218M in grants in FY2025). Deadline: invitation-based/rolling (Nonprofit Gateway Profile open year-round at cftexas.org/nonprofits).

title: Communities Foundation of Texas — W.W. Caruth Jr. Fund
description: Largest fund at CFT; funds visionary, large-scale efforts in public safety, health, and education. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Healthcare, Public Safety. Geographic scope: Regional (North Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits doing bold, large-scale work in North Texas; invitation-only. Award: $300M+ total granted since fund creation; large transformational grants. Deadline: invitation-only.

title: Chicago Community Trust — Discretionary Grants
description: Nation’s second-oldest community foundation (est. 1915); priority: closing wealth gap for Black and Latine Chicagoans. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Economic Mobility, Healthcare, Education. Geographic scope: Regional (Chicago region / Cook County). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or those with nonprofit fiscal sponsor serving Chicago region; apply via GrantCentral portal. Award: varies (multi-year grants common; $1.4B+ in DAF grants FY2023). Deadline: rolling (two open application periods annually; moving to rolling multi-cycle model Fall 2026; check cct.org/grants/how-to-apply).

title: Chicago Community Trust — Resilient Chicago Fund
description: Funds rethinking of safety-net service delivery amid federal funding changes; 231 applications received in first cycle. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Food Security, Healthcare, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Chicago / Cook County). Eligibility: Nonprofits based in Chicago/Cook County with experience in basic human needs systems. Award: Design/testing grants $100,000–$300,000; implementation grants multi-million-dollar, multi-year (up to 5 awards Fall 2026). Deadline: fixed date (first open call closed April 17, 2026; next cycle TBD Fall 2026; check cct.org/partnerships/rcf).

title: Chicago Community Trust — Young Leaders Fund
description: Donor group funding small start-up Chicago nonprofits in arts/culture, child development & education, and community & economic development. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Youth Programs, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Chicago city). Eligibility: Small, start-up Chicago nonprofits with operating budgets under $250,000; invite-only. Award: varies (30+ years; $1M+ total to 260+ nonprofits). Deadline: invitation-only (nominate via cct.org/young-leaders-fund).

title: Chicago Community Trust — Press Forward Chicago
description: Funds collaborations and shared infrastructure benefiting multiple local Chicago media outlets simultaneously. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building (local journalism/media). Geographic scope: Regional (Chicago city and nearby suburbs). Eligibility: Local independently owned or nonprofit news organizations in Chicago/suburbs; for-profits must have nonprofit fiscal sponsor. Award: varies (15% overhead cap). Deadline: fixed date (check cct.org for current RFP cycle).

title: New York Community Trust — Competitive Grants Program
description: Nation’s oldest community foundation still operating (est. 1924); 10 program areas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Education, Community Development, Environment, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (NYC, Long Island, Westchester; some national). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving NYC, Long Island, or Westchester. Award: $15,000–$25,000 typical competitive; $200M+ total annual grantmaking. Deadline: rolling (NYC proposals accepted anytime via Grantseeker Portal; Long Island and Westchester have specified windows; check thenytrust.org).

title: New York Community Trust — National Environment Program
description: Funds climate change mitigation, climate resilience, toxic pollutant protection, and urban ecology. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Environment. Geographic scope: National (US incl. territories). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits; national projects must have multi-state benefit. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (8-week RFP window in winter; 2026 window closed April 27; watch thenytrust.org for 2027 RFP, expected winter 2027).

title: New York Community Trust — Heisman Trophy Youth Development Fund
description: Supports organizations helping youth develop social, emotional, ethical, cognitive, and physical skills through integration of athletics and academics. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Sports & Recreation. Geographic scope: Regional (New York area). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits integrating athletics, academics, and youth development for ages 18 and under. Award: varies (annual RFP, typically spring). Deadline: annual (RFP issued early spring; check thenytrust.org for next 2027 cycle).
funder: The New York Community Trust / Heisman Trophy Trust

title: Community Foundation for Southeast Michigan — Community Grants Program
description: Major Midwest community foundation with 5 strategic focus areas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Healthcare, Community Development, Economic Mobility, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Southeast Michigan: Wayne, Oakland, Macomb, Monroe, Washtenaw, Livingston, St. Clair counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Southeast Michigan; two-stage process. Award: varies (multi-year grants common). Deadline: rolling (proposals accepted on rolling basis; submit via MyCF portal at cfsem.org).

title: Community Foundation for Southeast Michigan — Michigan Justice Fund
description: Funds innovative re-entry, workforce development, and decarceration strategies. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Michigan statewide). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or government agencies in Michigan focused on criminal justice reform and re-entry. Award: Planning grants up to $100,000; Implementation grants up to $400,000; Fellowship grants up to $6,000. Deadline: rolling/LOI-based (program reopening Fall 2026; submit LOI to mjf@cfsem.org; check cfsem.org).

title: Greater New Orleans Foundation — IMPACT Grants
description: Competitive grant program supporting nonprofits across Greater New Orleans. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Arts & Culture, Education, Environment, Healthcare. Geographic scope: Regional (Greater New Orleans / Southeast Louisiana). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or organizations with 501(c)(3) fiscal sponsor serving the Greater New Orleans region. Award: average $20,000. Deadline: annual (2026 cycle expected summer 2026; check gnof.org for next opening).

title: Greater New Orleans Foundation — IMPACT Special Funding
description: Targeted funding streams within IMPACT for health, youth development, arts, and environment. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Arts & Culture, Environment, Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (Greater New Orleans). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Greater New Orleans; one application per org per IMPACT cycle. Award: average $20,000 (named fund categories include Kahn Oppenheim Trust, Harold W. Newman Trust, Gulf States Eye Surgery Fund, Michael R. Boh Fund). Deadline: annual (opens July; deadline August 1 annually; check gnof.org for 2026 cycle).

title: Greater New Orleans Foundation — Urban Forest Tree Planting Grants
description: Supports New Orleans’ goal of planting 40,000 new trees by 2030. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Environment. Geographic scope: Regional (New Orleans). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits partnering with New Orleans Parks & Parkways on citywide tree planting. Award: varies. Deadline: rolling/project-based (check gnof.org for open cycles).

title: Greater New Orleans Foundation — Norco Community Fund
description: Place-based fund supporting nonprofits increasing capacity of the Norco, Louisiana community. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Norco, LA / Southeast Louisiana). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits addressing social and economic issues in the Norco community. Award: varies. Deadline: rolling (check gnof.org for open windows).

title: Arizona Community Foundation — Statewide Competitive Grants
description: Largest community foundation in Arizona; 6 regions statewide. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Health, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Arizona statewide). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, schools, faith-based orgs, tribal entities, government agencies in Arizona. Award: varies by regional cycle. Deadline: rolling/annual (regional cycles vary; check azfoundation.org for current open cycles).

title: Arizona Community Foundation — ACF of Flagstaff Grant Cycle
description: Two distinct regional grant cycles serving Flagstaff and rural northern Arizona communities. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Health. Geographic scope: Regional (Flagstaff, Page/Lake Powell, Tuba City, Williams, northern Arizona). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, schools, tribal entities serving northern Arizona. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (Flagstaff cycle historically May; check azfoundation.org for 2027 dates).

title: Community Foundation for Southern Arizona — CORE Grants
description: Trust-based, unrestricted general operating grants. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Southern Arizona). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in good standing for 2+ years, or fiscally sponsored organizations in Southern Arizona. Award: unrestricted general operating support ($9M+ awarded to 190+ orgs since 2017). Deadline: annual (applications open October 1 annually; check cfsaz.org for 2026–2027 cycle).

title: California Community Foundation — Competitive Grants
description: 100+ year-old LA County community foundation; $3.9B+ granted since 2004. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Healthcare, Housing, Immigration, Environment, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Los Angeles County). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in Los Angeles County. Award: varies ($342M in total grants FY2024). Deadline: rolling/open call (apply via NonprofitConnect portal at calfund.org; take eligibility test first).

title: California Community Foundation — Wildfire Recovery Fund: Community Healing & Restoration
description: Funds community and peer-based, culturally affirming services for wildfire survivors. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Los Angeles County wildfire-impacted communities). Eligibility: Nonprofits, grassroots groups, healing practitioners, community-led coalitions serving 2025 LA wildfire survivors. Award: varies ($30M+ raised for wildfire recovery). Deadline: fixed date (watch calfund.org for future rounds; first round closed Sept. 2025).

title: North Texas Community Foundation — ToolBox Grants
description: Funds talent, technology, and shared infrastructure to strengthen multiple Tarrant County nonprofits simultaneously. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Tarrant County, TX + surrounding 10 counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Tarrant County vulnerable populations; collaborative proposals strongly encouraged. Award: $20,000–$50,000 ($304,700 awarded to 9 orgs in 2026 cycle). Deadline: annual (watch northtexascf.org for 2027 cycle; 2026 cycle closed).

title: North Texas Community Foundation — Conservation & Environment Fund
description: Annual grants for programs ensuring access to the great outdoors and preserving open-space land in North Texas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Environment. Geographic scope: Regional (North Texas / Tarrant County). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits promoting environmental stewardship and open-space preservation in North Texas. Award: annual grants (amount varies). Deadline: annual (check northtexascf.org for cycle dates).

title: North Texas Community Foundation — Children’s Fund
description: General operating support for organizations providing critical services to abused and neglected children and families. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (Tarrant County, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) high-impact nonprofits serving victims of child abuse and neglect in Tarrant County. Award: general operating support (varies). Deadline: annual (check northtexascf.org for cycle dates).

title: North Texas Community Foundation — Animal Welfare Grants
description: Supports local animal welfare organizations in the North Texas area. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: Regional (Tarrant County / North Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) local animal welfare organizations serving North Texas. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (check northtexascf.org for cycle dates).

title: The Dallas Foundation — Spring & Summer Grant Cycles
description: Texas’s oldest community foundation (est. 1929); multiple named fund cycles covering arts, animal welfare, domestic violence, health, and disability. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Animal Welfare, Healthcare, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (Dallas County, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Dallas County. Award: $10,000–$50,000 ($375,000 for animal welfare; $160,000 for arts per cycle). Deadline: UPCOMING — Summer cycle: June 10–26 annually (2026 Summer cycle application window check dallasfoundation.org; Winter cycle Jan 14–28).

title: The Dallas Foundation — Animal Welfare Grants
description: Supports prevention of cruelty, veterinary care, spay/neuter, pet ownership education, shelter support, and adoption in Dallas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Animal Welfare. Geographic scope: Regional (Dallas County, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) animal welfare nonprofits serving Dallas County. Award: $10,000–$50,000 (approx. $375,000 total per cycle via 4 named funds). Deadline: biannual (Jan. 14–28 Winter; June 10–26 Summer; check dallasfoundation.org for next window).

title: The Dallas Foundation — Arts & Culture Grants
description: Funds organizations that expand arts access, foster creative expression, and preserve cultural traditions in Dallas. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Dallas County, TX). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) performing and visual arts organizations serving Dallas County. Award: $10,000–$50,000 (approx. $160,000 total per cycle). Deadline: biannual (Jan. 14–28 Winter; June 10–26 Summer; check dallasfoundation.org for next window).

title: Dallas Stars Foundation — Community Grant Program
description: Funds local nonprofits through in-game 50/50 raffle proceeds; focuses on youth hockey, community engagement, and children’s health. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Sports & Recreation, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (North Texas / Dallas-Fort Worth). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in North Texas; prior season recipients ineligible. Award: average ~$10,000 (via 50/50 Raffle proceeds). Deadline: annual (applications open each fall; check nhl.com/stars/community/grants for 2026–2027 season cycle).

title: Dallas Stars Foundation — Special Project Grants
description: In-kind grants to refurbish or replace existing play or learning spaces, courts, or gyms across North Texas. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Sports & Recreation, Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (North Texas). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits and community organizations in North Texas with existing play/learning space needing refurbishment. Award: in-kind (renovation/refurbishment of play spaces, courts, gyms). Deadline: annual (check nhl.com/stars/community/grants for current cycle).

title: AZ Blue Foundation — Mental Health Grant
description: Each year identifies a specific mental health need in Arizona and awards a single $100,000 grant. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: Regional (Arizona statewide). Eligibility: Arizona-based nonprofits, academic institutions, and government entities with active Arizona Community Foundation account. Award: $100,000 (one targeted grant per year). Deadline: annual (applications accepted Feb.–Mar. annually; check azbluefoundation.org for 2027 cycle).
funder: AZ Blue Foundation (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Arizona)

title: Google Ad Grants
description: 14 billion+ website visits driven for nonprofits since inception; now includes Performance Max campaigns. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) US nonprofits in good standing; not hospitals, schools, or government entities; must have working HTTPS website. Award: $10,000/month in Google Search ad credits (ongoing, no expiration). Deadline: rolling (apply anytime via google.com/nonprofits; approval 2–14 days).

title: Google.org Impact Challenge: Generative AI Accelerator
description: Funds generative AI solutions for knowledge/skills, resilient societies, or applied science. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: STEM & Technology, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: National/Global. Eligibility: Nonprofits, civic entities, academic institutions, social enterprises with gen AI-powered social impact solutions. Award: $500,000–$2,000,000+ (share of $30M pool; includes 6 months accelerator support, Google Cloud credits, pro bono assistance). Deadline: periodic open calls (watch google.org for next cohort; part of $60M+ Google.org AI commitment 2025–2026).

title: Google.org Impact Challenge: AI for Government Innovation
description: Funds AI-powered improvements to healthcare access, crisis resilience, and economic infrastructure via government partnerships. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: STEM & Technology, Community Development, Healthcare. Geographic scope: National/Global. Eligibility: Nonprofits, social enterprises, academic institutions partnering with government agencies to deploy AI for public services. Award: $1,000,000–$3,000,000 (share of $30M global pool). Deadline: fixed date (applications closed April 3, 2026; watch google.org for next cycle).

title: MacArthur Foundation — 100&Change Competition
description: Single $100M award for any proposed solution to any critical problem; open to any sector. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Environment, Healthcare (any critical problem). Geographic scope: National/Global. Eligibility: Nonprofits and qualifying for-profit organizations; no geographic restriction. Award: $100,000,000 (single grant; one winner per cycle every 3 years). Deadline: periodic (competition cycle repeats every 3 years; watch macfound.org for next open call).
funder: John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation

title: MacArthur Foundation — Journalism & Media Enduring Commitment
description: Long-standing commitment to explanatory and investigative journalism, press freedom, and local news infrastructure. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture (local/investigative journalism). Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: National and regional nonprofit journalism organizations; press freedom organizations; submit LOI via macfound.org eligibility quiz. Award: varies (multi-year strategic grants). Deadline: rolling/invitation-based.
funder: John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation

title: MacArthur Foundation — Chicago Commitment (Leaders for a New Chicago)
description: MacArthur’s hometown commitment strengthening Chicago through leadership development, arts, and community investment. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education. Geographic scope: Regional (Chicago, IL). Eligibility: Chicago-based nonprofits and individuals through Leaders for a New Chicago program. Award: varies (part of sustained Chicago investment; $9.19B+ in grants since 1978). Deadline: invitation-based (check macfound.org for current Leaders for a New Chicago cycle).
funder: John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation

title: Community Foundation for Greater Atlanta — Grants to Black-Led Organizations
description: Dedicated grantmaking building capacity of Black-led organizations in metro Atlanta; part of CFGA’s broader commitment to racial equity and organizational sustainability. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (11-county metro Atlanta, GA). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits led by and primarily serving Black communities in metro Atlanta; must operate in 11-county area. Award: general operating support (varies; part of CFGA’s $195M annual grantmaking). Deadline: annual (2026 application window expected fall 2026; check cfgreateratlanta.org/grant-opportunities for opening date or contact grants@cfgreateratlanta.org).

title: Community Foundation for Greater Atlanta — Metropolitan Atlanta Arts Grant
description: General operating support for independent metro Atlanta arts organizations. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (11-county metro Atlanta, GA). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) arts organizations with 70%+ programming in arts/culture, operating expenses $25,000–$2M, active year-round, based in metro Atlanta. Award: $10,000–$75,000 typical; general operating support. Deadline: annual (watch for 2027 cycle opening in early spring 2027; check cfgreateratlanta.org).

title: Community Foundation for Greater Atlanta — Housing Portfolio Grants
description: Strategic housing grants focusing on stability services and policy advocacy for metro Atlanta residents. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development (affordable housing). Geographic scope: Regional (5-county core: Clayton, Cobb, DeKalb, Fulton, Gwinnett, GA). Eligibility: Nonprofit housing developers and affordable housing service providers; new orgs apply by July 1, 2026 (rolling through Sept. 30, 2026). Award: varies (2026 focus: housing stability, policy/advocacy). Deadline: Rolling — new applicants accepted through September 30, 2026 (submit by July 1, 2026 preferred; accepted through Sept. 30, 2026 at cfgreateratlanta.org/grant-opportunities).

title: Community Foundation for Greater Atlanta — Extra Wish Catalog
description: Annual giving catalog connecting donor-advised fundholders to small/mid Atlanta nonprofits; 2026 edition focused on general operating requests. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (metro Atlanta, GA). Eligibility: Small to mid-sized nonprofits (budget $25,000–$3M) serving metro Atlanta; general operating requests. Award: varies (annual end-of-year giving catalog shared with 1,000+ CFGA fundholders). Deadline: annual (submissions open fall/winter 2026; check cfgreateratlanta.org).

title: Arizona Community Foundation — ACF of Yavapai County Grant Cycle
description: Annual winter grant cycle for nonprofits in Prescott and Sedona-area Yavapai County. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Health. Geographic scope: Regional (Yavapai County, AZ). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, schools, tribal entities serving Yavapai County. Award: varies. Deadline: annual (historically January–February; check azfoundation.org for 2027 cycle).

title: Arizona Community Foundation — ACF of Sedona Grant Cycle
description: Regional grant cycle for nonprofits in Sedona and the Verde Valley. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Arts & Culture, Education. Geographic scope: Regional (Sedona and the Verde Valley, AZ). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, schools, tribal entities; one application per EIN per cycle. Award: varies ($19M+ awarded to Sedona-area nonprofits since 1991). Deadline: annual (check azfoundation.org for 2026–2027 cycle dates).

title: AZ Blue Foundation — Health Equity Grants (Invitation-Only)
description: Targeted health equity funding for Arizona organizations; administered through Arizona Community Foundation portal. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: Regional (Arizona statewide). Eligibility: Arizona-based nonprofits, academic institutions, recognized government entities invited by AZ Blue Foundation staff; must have active ACF account. Award: varies. Deadline: invitation-only (check azbluefoundation.org for any open calls; contact grants@azbluefoundation.org).
funder: AZ Blue Foundation (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Arizona)

title: Inland Empire Community Foundation — Community Impact Fund
description: IECF’s only unrestricted grantmaking; nonprofit capacity amid federal funding changes. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Youth Programs, Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Inland Empire, CA: Riverside and San Bernardino counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in good standing serving the Inland Empire; $500,000+ annual operating budget and 2+ years operating. Award: unrestricted general operating support ($500,000 distributed in 2025 cycle). Deadline: annual (2025 cycle closed; 2026 cycle expected fall 2026; check iegives.org).

title: Communities Foundation of Texas — Nonprofit Gateway / Open Grantmaking
description: Invitation-based across 200+ named funds. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Healthcare, Arts & Culture, Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (North Texas / Dallas metro). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits with 2+ years active designation serving North Texas; complete Nonprofit Gateway Profile. Award: varies (CFT distributed $218M in grants FY2025). Deadline: rolling/invitation-based (Nonprofit Gateway Profile open year-round at cftexas.org/nonprofits).

title: W.K. Kellogg Foundation — General Grantmaking (Early Childhood & Family Economic Security)
description: One of the largest US foundations ($9B+ assets); funds early childhood education, maternal/infant health, food systems, jobs, and racial equity. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Food Security, Healthcare, Education. Geographic scope: National (priority: Michigan, Mississippi, New Mexico, New Orleans, LA). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, public/governmental entities, schools, hospitals, religious institutions. Award: $7,000–$200,000+ (typical multi-year $50,000–$300,000; $262M+ in commitments FY2024). Deadline: rolling (submit LOI anytime via Fluxx at wkkf.org/grantseekers; hear back within 30 business days).

title: W.K. Kellogg Foundation — Food Systems & Agriculture Grants
description: Supports strong and equitable food systems, food access, and generational wealth creation through agriculture. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Food Security. Geographic scope: National (priority: Michigan, Mississippi, New Mexico, New Orleans). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, tribal organizations, public entities focused on equitable food systems. Award: $150,000–$200,000 (recent grants: $200,000 over 2 years). Deadline: rolling (submit LOI via Fluxx at wkkf.org).

title: W.K. Kellogg Foundation — Health & Health Equity Grants
description: Funds expanding access to primary healthcare, maternal and infant health, and community-based mental health. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare. Geographic scope: National (priority: Michigan, Mississippi, New Mexico, Louisiana). Eligibility: Community health centers, FQHCs, maternal/child health nonprofits, local public health orgs. Award: $150,000–$300,000. Deadline: rolling (submit LOI anytime via Fluxx at wkkf.org/grantseekers).

title: Oregon Community Foundation — Baker County Fund
description: Dedicated place-based fund supporting nonprofits in Baker County, Oregon’s rural eastern region; one of the few OCF grant programs with a summer application window. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Baker County and North Powder, OR). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, tribal entities, and government entities supporting Baker County and the town of North Powder, Oregon. Award: general operating support (varies). Deadline: UPCOMING — June 1–July 15, 2026 annually (confirmed on oregoncf.org/grants-and-scholarships/grants; window currently open through July 15, 2026).

title: Oregon Community Foundation — Arts & Culture Grants (Artists’ Field Fellowship)
description: Fields Fellowship invests in Oregon artists and communities; $7M awarded in second wave 2026. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Oregon statewide). Eligibility: Oregon artists via LOI; organizational partners are Oregon nonprofits or public entities. Award: varies (OCF invested $7M in Arts & Culture in 2026). Deadline: annual LOI window (Jan.–Feb. annually; check oregoncf.org for 2027 LOI window).

title: Oregon Community Foundation — Walker Fund (Southern Oregon)
description: Named fund supporting Southern Oregon communities; $2.25M distributed in 2026. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Environment. Geographic scope: Regional (Southern Oregon: Jackson, Josephine, Klamath, Douglas counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Southern Oregon communities. Award: varies (nearly 75 Southern Oregon nonprofits received $2.25M in June 2026). Deadline: annual (check oregoncf.org for application window; apply in spring).

title: Oregon Community Foundation — Food Security Fund
description: OCF and donors committed $1.9M to organizations feeding hungry Oregonians in 2025. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Food Security. Geographic scope: Regional (Oregon statewide). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits and organizations serving hungry Oregonians. Award: varies ($1.9M committed to food security organizations in Nov. 2025). Deadline: rolling/annual (check oregoncf.org for open food security grant windows).

title: Doris Duke Foundation — Environment Program Grants
description: Funds conservation of biodiversity, land trusts, natural climate solutions, indigenous conservation. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Environment. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: US 501(c)(3) nonprofits focused on biodiversity, land conservation, natural climate solutions, and indigenous-led conservation; unsolicited LOIs accepted at grants@dorisduke.org. Award: $100,000–$1,000,000 (average ~$100,000; $62.24M in total grants in 2024). Deadline: invitation-based/periodic RFPs (submit unsolicited LOI to grants@dorisduke.org; check dorisduke.org for open calls).

title: Doris Duke Foundation — Performing Arts Program (Leadership Grants)
description: Supports organizations’ long-term goals in dance, jazz, and theater. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: US 501(c)(3) performing arts organizations in dance, jazz, and theater; not visual arts, opera, symphony, classical ballet, or arts education. Award: multi-year grants ($100,000–$1,000,000 range). Deadline: invitation-based (check dorisduke.org for any open RFP competitions via intermediary organizations).

title: Doris Duke Foundation — Child Well-Being Program
description: Funds prevention-oriented child welfare organizations; past grantees in Kentucky, Oregon, South Carolina, San Francisco. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Local, regional nonprofits and state agencies doing preventive work to meet family needs before child maltreatment; primarily invitation-based. Award: $100,000–$1,000,000 (multi-year). Deadline: invitation-based (submit LOI to grants@dorisduke.org if work aligns).

title: Doris Duke Foundation — Building Bridges Program
description: Works to elevate U.S. Muslim storytelling and build greater mutual understanding through arts, fellowships, and narrative change. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Community Development. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: US 501(c)(3) nonprofits elevating Muslim storytelling and advancing cross-cultural understanding. Award: $100,000–$1,000,000 (multi-year). Deadline: invitation-based/periodic (submit LOI to grants@dorisduke.org).

title: Bank of America Neighborhood Builders Program
description: Flagship Bank of America philanthropy; $400,000 plus executive leadership development in 40+ US markets; 2,700+ nonprofits funded since 2004. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: National (40+ US cities). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in housing/community development OR workforce development/economic mobility; operating budget $1M–$40M; nominated by BofA employees or community partners. Award: $400,000 over 2 years ($200,000/year) plus leadership training. Deadline: annual (nominations open each spring; 2026 applications closed June 22; 2027 cycle expected spring 2027 — watch about.bankofamerica.com/en/making-an-impact/neighborhood-builders).

title: Bank of America Grants — Arts & Culture
description: Funds museums, performing arts organizations, and cultural institutions; Art Conservation Project grants fund preservation of artworks. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: National (markets where BofA operates). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) arts and cultural nonprofits in markets where Bank of America operates. Award: varies (part of $250M+ annual charitable giving). Deadline: rolling/invitation-based (contact local BofA market philanthropy officer or check about.bankofamerica.com).

title: Texas Commission on the Arts — Organizational Support Program (OSP)
description: TCA’s largest grant program; unrestricted general operating support for established Texas arts organizations. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Statewide (Texas). Eligibility: Texas-based 501(c)(3) arts organizations with 3+ consecutive years arts programming, $50,000+ operating expenses. Award: $5,000–$200,000 (general operating support; tiered by operating expenses). Deadline: annual (FY2027 cycle expected fall 2026; check arts.texas.gov for exact date).

title: Texas Commission on the Arts — Jump! Grants
description: Entry-level TCA grant for smaller and newer Texas arts organizations. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Statewide (Texas). Eligibility: Texas-based 501(c)(3) nonprofits or public schools with at least 1 year arts programming and $10,000–$49,999 operating expenses. Award: $1,000–$10,000. Deadline: annual (FY2027 expected fall 2026; check arts.texas.gov).

title: Cleveland Foundation — Immediate Impact Grants (Wellbeing & Critical Needs)
description: World’s first community foundation (est. 1914); $195M+ granted in 2025 to 2,221 orgs. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Cuyahoga, Lake, and Geauga counties, OH). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Greater Cleveland; register via Grants Gateway portal. Award: $3,000–$2,400,000 (331 grants totaling $31.5M in 2025; average $95,000). Deadline: rolling (apply via Grants Gateway portal year-round at clevelandfoundation.org/grants/apply-for-a-grant).

title: Cleveland Foundation — Long-Term Impact: Grow Our Region
description: Includes Good Jobs Fund for brownfield cleanup and industrial revitalization. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, STEM & Technology. Geographic scope: Regional (Greater Cleveland / Northeast Ohio). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits and collaboratives advancing economic vitality in Northeast Ohio. Award: varies ($11.6M in 2025 across grants in this category). Deadline: rolling/invitation-based (apply via Grants Gateway; long-term initiatives often proactively developed with partners).

title: Cleveland Foundation — Arts & Culture Grants (via Grants Gateway)
description: Cleveland Foundation’s rolling arts and culture grantmaking; funds performing arts, visual arts, museums, cultural institutions, and arts education across Greater Cleveland. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Cuyahoga, Lake, Geauga counties, OH). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) arts and cultural nonprofits serving Greater Cleveland; apply via Grants Gateway portal year-round. Award: $3,000–$500,000 (arts and culture is one of 5 core program areas; part of $195M+ annual grantmaking). Deadline: Rolling — apply anytime via Grants Gateway portal (clevelandfoundation.org/grants/apply-for-a-grant; no fixed deadline; standard application open year-round).

title: Saint Paul & Minnesota Foundation — Minnesota Community IDEAS Program
description: Bush Foundation-partnered statewide grant; unrestricted $125,000 grants; no reporting required; community-led design. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Minnesota statewide + 11 Native Nations). Eligibility: Nonprofits and organizations in Minnesota or 11 Native Nations; check grant guidelines at spmcf.org. Award: $125,000 per grant (9 grants in 2026; $1.6M/year 2025–2027; unrestricted). Deadline: annual (2026 cycle closed July; watch spmcf.org for 2027 cycle interest form opening January 2027).

title: Saint Paul & Minnesota Foundation — Community Solutions Grant Program
description: Rolling-deadline grant program from Minnesota’s largest community foundation. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Healthcare. Geographic scope: Regional (East Metro Minnesota: Ramsey, Washington, Dakota counties + statewide). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, government entities, educational institutions, or orgs with eligible fiscal sponsor. Award: varies (new 2026 grant programs launched with F.R. Bigelow Foundation and Mardag Foundation). Deadline: rolling (open rolling application dates; check spmcf.org for current windows).

title: Southern Minnesota Initiative Foundation — Economic, Community & Workforce Development Grants
description: Funds early-stage, innovative economic and workforce development efforts across 20 counties and one Native nation in Southern Minnesota. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Southern Minnesota: 20-county region + 1 Native nation). Eligibility: Nonprofits, local governments, schools in SMIF’s 20-county southern Minnesota region; pilot/innovative projects preferred. Award: varies (competitive; additional round in 2026). Deadline: UPCOMING — August 10, 2026 at 4 p.m. (confirmed on smifoundation.org for summer 2026 round; check smifoundation.org for 2027 dates).

title: Southern Minnesota Initiative Foundation — Small Town Grants
description: Funds collaborative initiatives improving the culture, education, economy, and social fabric of small Southern Minnesota towns. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Regional (Southern Minnesota communities under 10,000 population). Eligibility: Communities under 10,000 population in SMIF’s 20-county region; 2026 priority: initiatives that lift up marginalized community members. Award: varies (in-kind paint/coating also available for murals and public art). Deadline: UPCOMING — opens July 22, 2026 (check smifoundation.org for full cycle details).

title: Community Foundation Tampa Bay — Annual Competitive Grants
description: Tampa Bay’s largest annual competitive grant; 5 focus areas; record 363 applications in 2025. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Healthcare, Education, Environment, Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (Hillsborough, Pinellas, Pasco, Hernando, Citrus counties, FL). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits with at least 1 year operational history and IRS Form 990 filed; must serve at least one of 5 listed Florida counties. Award: $10,000–$50,000 (84 orgs funded totaling $2.2M+ in 2025; 363 applications received). Deadline: annual (2027 cycle expected to open December 2026; LOI due ~Jan. 7, full application ~March 2, notifications ~May 22 — check cftampabay.org).

title: Community Foundation Tampa Bay — Critical Needs List
description: Innovative rolling program connecting nonprofits facing emergency operational needs with Community Foundation fundholders for rapid response. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Hillsborough, Pinellas, Pasco, Hernando, Citrus counties, FL). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving Tampa Bay 5-county area with urgent, unbudgeted, time-sensitive need. Award: varies (matched 1:3 by fundholders; min. $250 triggers $750 match; max. $5,000 triggers $15,000 match). Deadline: Rolling — open year-round (submit Critical Needs Form at cftampabay.org/grants anytime).

title: Community Foundation Tampa Bay — Smith & Associates Real Estate Foundation Grant
description: Named fund within Community Foundation Tampa Bay; focuses on housing, community vibrancy, and positive education/mental wellbeing. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Healthcare (mental health/wellbeing), Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (Hillsborough and Pinellas counties, FL). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in Hillsborough and Pinellas counties working in housing, community vibrancy, or positive education/mental wellbeing. Award: varies (annual competitive process). Deadline: annual (timeline announced on cftampabay.org each year).
funder: Community Foundation Tampa Bay / Smith & Associates Real Estate Foundation

title: Initiative Foundation (Central MN) — Emerging Needs & Nonprofit Capacity-Building Grants
description: Dual-purpose grant combining immediate stabilization support with longer-term capacity building. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Capacity Building. Geographic scope: Regional (Central Minnesota: 14 counties + 2 Native nations). Eligibility: Nonprofits, local units of government, schools in Initiative Foundation’s 14-county Central Minnesota region. Award: two complementary grants in one (short-term stabilization + long-term organizational strengthening); amounts vary. Deadline: annual (one additional grant round in 2026; check ifound.org for date).

title: Texas Commission on the Arts — Touring Grants
description: Subsidizes fees for Texas arts presenters booking artists from the official TCA Texas Touring Roster. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture. Geographic scope: Statewide (Texas). Eligibility: Texas nonprofits or public entities presenting touring artists/ensembles from the TCA Texas Touring Roster. Award: $1,500–$7,500 per engagement. Deadline: rolling (applications accepted at least 6 weeks before the engagement; check arts.texas.gov for current guidelines).

title: Texas Commission on the Arts — Arts Respond: Public Art
description: Funds public art projects that strengthen communities; includes murals, sculptures, and temporary installations. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Community Development. Geographic scope: Statewide (Texas). Eligibility: Texas 501(c)(3) nonprofits, units of local government, and K-12 schools with public art projects; Texas-based artists must be contracted. Award: $5,000–$50,000. Deadline: annual (FY2027 expected fall 2026; check arts.texas.gov).

title: Permian Basin Area Foundation — Community Impact Grants
description: West Texas community foundation serving the oil-rich Permian Basin. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Education, Arts & Culture, Community Development, Healthcare, Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (Permian Basin: Midland, Odessa, and surrounding West Texas counties). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits serving the Permian Basin region of West Texas. Award: varies (multiple named funds; check pbaf.org for current cycle). Deadline: annual (check pbaf.org for current grant cycle dates).

title: El Paso Community Foundation — Competitive Grants
description: Binational community foundation serving the El Paso–Juárez–Las Cruces region; 700+ funds. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Arts & Culture, Education, Community Development, Healthcare, Environment. Geographic scope: Regional (El Paso County, TX + Doña Ana County, NM + Ciudad Juárez, Mexico). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits or governmental entities serving El Paso County and/or Doña Ana County. Award: varies ($28M+ distributed since 1977 via 700+ funds). Deadline: annual (check epcf.org/grant-programs for current cycle; multiple named fund cycles throughout year).

title: Microsoft for Nonprofits — Technology Grant Program
description: Microsoft’s most accessible nonprofit program; product grants worth thousands annually. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: STEM & Technology, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: 501(c)(3) US nonprofits in good standing; not hospitals, schools, or government entities; must have mission aligned with Microsoft’s philanthropic goals. Award: Azure cloud credits up to $3,500/year; Microsoft 365 Business Premium at 75% discount; GitHub for Nonprofits (free); LinkedIn nonprofit discounts; Microsoft 365 Copilot access. Deadline: rolling (apply anytime via microsoft.com/en-us/nonprofits; 450,000+ nonprofits globally approved).

title: Microsoft Philanthropies — AI for Humanitarian Action
description: $40M+ initiative funding AI-powered solutions for disaster response, refugee services, human rights, and programs for women and children. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: STEM & Technology, Healthcare, Community Development. Geographic scope: National/Global. Eligibility: Nonprofits and humanitarian organizations focused on disaster response, refugees/displaced people, human rights, or needs of women and children; cash grants by invitation only. Award: $500,000–$5,000,000+ (part of $40M+ five-year initiative; 29 projects funded). Deadline: invitation-only (watch microsoft.com/en-us/corporate-responsibility for any open RFPs).

title: Microsoft Employee Giving Match Program
description: One of the most generous corporate employee matching programs in the US; register your nonprofit at Microsoft’s giving platform. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Healthcare. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Any IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) US nonprofit; must be registered on Microsoft’s giving platform. Award: up to $15,000/year per employee in matching (1:1 match + $25/volunteer hour); $255.6M+ matched by employees in 2025 supporting 36,500 nonprofits. Deadline: rolling (employees can direct matches year-round; October is Microsoft’s Giving Campaign month).

title: Annie E. Casey Foundation — Child Welfare & Family Strengthening Grants
description: One of the nation’s largest child-focused foundations ($3.4B+ assets); funds family preservation, foster care reform, kinship care, juvenile justice alternatives. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Healthcare, Community Development. Geographic scope: National (US including Puerto Rico and USVI). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, public agencies, research institutions with demonstrated work in child welfare, family strengthening, or juvenile justice; invitation-only. Award: $50,000–$500,000+ (average grant $74,857 in 2025; $128.9M+ total in 2025 across 1,526 grants). Deadline: invitation-only (watch aecf.org/grant-making for any open calls).

title: Annie E. Casey Foundation — Thrive by 25 Initiative
description: Ten-year initiative dedicating 50%+ of AECF grantmaking to youth 14–24. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Education, Community Development. Geographic scope: National (priority: Albuquerque NM, Baltimore MD, Atlanta GA, and other Thrive by 25 cities). Eligibility: Nonprofits and public agencies serving young people ages 14–24 in designated Thrive by 25 communities; by invitation and through collaborative funding partnerships. Award: $50,000–$100,000 (renewable; $2.1M committed with 13 philanthropic partners in NM alone). Deadline: invitation-based/collaborative (watch aecf.org for open calls within Thrive by 25 communities).

title: JPMorgan Chase Foundation — Workforce Readiness Grants
description: Nation’s largest bank-affiliated foundation ($1.22B assets; $315M+ annual grants); funds workforce readiness, small business growth, financial capability, and community development. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Community Development, Education (workforce development). Geographic scope: National (priority: CA, TX, IL, NY + 40+ cities). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) workforce training nonprofits, CDFIs, career-connected education orgs with strong job placement rates; invitation-only. Award: $5,000–$5,000,000 (average ~$34,672; large strategic partners receive multi-year grants; $315M+ in annual grants). Deadline: invitation-only (contact jpmcfoundation@jpmorgan.com; watch for occasional RFPs through CDFI intermediaries at jpmorganchase.com/impact).

title: JPMorgan Chase Foundation — AdvancingCities Initiative
description: Combines $500M in lending capital, philanthropic capital, and firm expertise for affordable housing, small business, and economic mobility in selected US cities. Funder type: corporate. Focus areas: Community Development. Geographic scope: National (selected US cities: Chicago, Louisville, Miami, San Diego, Syracuse, Baton Rouge, New Orleans, Detroit, Washington DC). Eligibility: Nonprofits and civic organizations in selected AdvancingCities cities; competitive challenge process. Award: $3,000,000–$5,000,000 per selected city (part of $500M total commitment). Deadline: periodic competitive challenge (check jpmorganchase.com/impact/philanthropy for announcements).

title: William T. Grant Foundation — Research Grants
description: Funds rigorous research on reducing inequality in youth outcomes. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Education. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Researchers and organizations studying programs, policies, and practices that improve outcomes for young people ages 5–25 in the US. Award: $100,000–$600,000 (major research grants up to $600,000 over 2–3 years). Deadline: biannual (LOI cycles: Spring and Fall; Fall 2026 LOI deadline expected September 2026 — check wtgrantfoundation.org).

title: William T. Grant Foundation — Institutional Challenge Grant
description: Challenges institutions to build sustained capacity for using research evidence in decisions affecting youth. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Youth Programs, Education, Capacity Building. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Research universities and nonprofits with research capacity working to reduce inequality in youth outcomes; selective, invitation-based after exploratory discussion. Award: up to $650,000 over 3 years. Deadline: invitation-based (contact wtgrantfoundation.org to initiate conversation).

title: EPA Environmental Justice Collaborative Problem-Solving (EJCPS) Grants
description: EPA’s primary direct grant to community nonprofits; funds collaborative solutions to local environmental and public health problems. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Environment, Healthcare, Community Development. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Community-based nonprofit 501(c)(3) organizations; must be in or working directly with an overburdened community facing environmental/health challenges. Award: $150,000–$500,000 per award (cooperative agreements; 1–3 year projects). Deadline: annual (check epa.gov/environmentaljustice for current NOFO on grants.gov; watch for FY2026/FY2027 NOFO announcement).

title: EPA Environmental Justice Government-to-Government (EJG2G) Program
description: Helps state, local, and tribal governments build capacity to integrate environmental justice; nonprofits participate as community partners and sub-recipients. Funder type: government. Focus areas: Environment, Community Development. Geographic scope: National (tribal and local governments; nonprofits as sub-recipients). Eligibility: State, local, and tribal governments; nonprofits can be sub-recipients/partners. Award: up to $200,000 per award (cooperative agreements; 1–2 years). Deadline: annual (check epa.gov/environmentaljustice for current NOFO).

title: Conrad N. Hilton Foundation — Grants (Homelessness, Water, Early Childhood)
description: Major foundation ($695M+ assets) funding solutions to homelessness, safe water access, early childhood development, foster youth, and Catholic sisters. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Healthcare, Food Security, Community Development. Geographic scope: National/Global (US nonprofits; priority: homelessness in Los Angeles, early childhood dev). Eligibility: US 501(c)(3) nonprofits and global NGOs; primarily invitation-based; unsolicited LOIs accepted in limited circumstances — check hiltonfoundation.org for current open opportunities. Award: $100,000–$5,000,000+ (average $350,000+; $75M+ annually). Deadline: invitation-based (check hiltonfoundation.org/grants/how-to-apply for current open opportunities).

title: Conrad N. Hilton Foundation — Chronic Homelessness Initiative
description: Hilton Foundation’s largest domestic program; funds permanent supportive housing, homelessness prevention, family shelter, and systems-change for chronic homelessness. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Healthcare. Geographic scope: Regional (Los Angeles County, CA; national policy). Eligibility: Los Angeles-area nonprofits and national policy organizations working on chronic and family homelessness solutions; invitation-based. Award: $500,000–$5,000,000 (large strategic multi-year grants; $200M+ committed to homelessness work over decade). Deadline: invitation-based (contact hiltonfoundation.org).

title: Lumina Foundation — Grants (Postsecondary Education)
description: Indianapolis-based foundation solely focused on increasing postsecondary education attainment; $1.3B+ assets; priority to historically underrepresented students. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Education. Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Nonprofits, colleges/universities, policy organizations, and research institutions working on postsecondary attainment, credential quality, or equitable higher education access. Award: $50,000–$3,000,000+ (average ~$300,000; $1.3B+ in assets; $50M+ annually). Deadline: rolling/invitation-based (submit LOI at luminafoundation.org/grants).

title: Lumina Foundation — Adult Learner and Workforce Credentials
description: Funds innovations in stackable credentials, apprenticeships, short-term workforce training, prior learning assessment, and adult learner support. Funder type: foundation. Focus areas: Education, Community Development (workforce). Geographic scope: National. Eligibility: Nonprofits, community colleges, workforce development orgs, state agencies advancing short-term credentials and adult learner pathways. Award: $50,000–$2,000,000 (multi-year grants; part of $50M+ annual grantmaking). Deadline: rolling/invitation-based (check luminafoundation.org/grants for current open calls; LOI accepted year-round).

title: The Denver Foundation — Community Grants Program
description: Denver’s largest community foundation (est. 1925; $1.6B+ granted in 100 years); 4 priority areas: economic opportunity, environment/climate, housing, and youth well-being. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development, Education, Environment, Youth Programs. Geographic scope: Regional (7-county Metro Denver: Adams, Arapahoe, Boulder, Broomfield, Denver, Douglas, Jefferson, CO). Eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits, faith communities, public school districts, units of government, and fiscally sponsored orgs serving Metro Denver; at least 12 months between applications. Award: $20,000–$50,000 ($6.1M awarded to 150 nonprofits in 2025; ~500 proposals received). Deadline: biannual (Cycle 2 opens June 15, closes August 3, 2026 at 5pm MDT — check denverfoundation.org/our-grants-program for confirmation; Cycle 1 Feb–mid-March).

title: The Denver Foundation — Strengthening Neighborhoods Program
description: Small grassroots grants for community-led projects improving neighborhoods in Metro Denver. Funder type: community foundation. Focus areas: Community Development. Geographic scope: Regional (7-county Metro Denver, CO). Eligibility: Community groups and nonprofits in Metro Denver; collaborative groups of residents, orgs, schools, and businesses eligible. Award: up to $5,000 (average $2,500). Deadline: rolling (open year-round; check denverfoundation.org/funding-opportunities).

title: The Denver Foundation — Capacity Building Fund
